ref: “A virgin will have a baby boy, and he will be called Immanuel,” which means “God is with us.”
text: “Pɔ'asarisɔ nin ti gbaai puuwa n‑dɔ'ai bidibga, Ka ba ti booli u yuuri Imanuɛl.”

ref: From David to the time of the exile in Babylonia, the ancestors of Jesus were: David, Solomon , Rehoboam, Abijah, Asa, Jehoshaphat, Jehoram; Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, Hezekiah, Manasseh, Amon, Josiah, and Jehoiachin and his brothers.
text: Ka ŋɔn NaaGyoosaaya biisi n daa nyɛ NaaGyɛkɔnaaya ni u tunzɔppa: lala saha ka ba daa gbaasi ba n‑kyaŋŋi Babɛlɔn.

ref: From the exile to the birth of Jesus, his ancestors were: Jehoiachin, Shealtiel, Zerubbabel, Abiud, Eliakim, Azor, Zadok, Achim; Eliud, Eleazar, Matthan, Jacob, and Joseph, the husband of Mary, the mother of Jesus, who is called the Messiah.
text: Ŋɔn Yaakubu maa bii n daa nyɛ Gyoosɛfi; ka Gyoosɛfi mun daa nyɛ Mariama sira - ŋɔn Mariama mun daa dɔ'ai Yisa, sɔ ban boonni ‘Kirisita, Masiiya’ la.

ref: more to the birth of the Messiah. The Birth of Jesus (Luke
text: Eezɔr bii daa nyɛ Zeedɔkku, ka Zeedɔkku mun bii daa nyɛ Eekyim, ka Eekyim bii daa nyɛ Ilayuuda;

ref: This is how Jesus Christ was born. A young woman named Mary was engaged to Joseph from King David's family. But before they were married, she learned that she was going to have a baby by God's Holy Spirit.
text: Tɔ. Ban daa dɔ'ai Yisa Masiiya seem nŋɔa#:- Sahaseaa ban daa zaŋŋi u ma Mariama n‑ti Gyoosɛfi, ka na bu taasi u la, di saha ka ba daa yi nya ka Naawunni Sii Kasi yiiko daa pun kyɛ ka pɔ'asariga maa mari puuwa.

ref: Joseph was a good man and did not want to embarrass Mary in front of everyone. So he decided to quietly call off the wedding.
text: U sira Gyoosɛfi daa nyɛ la ninvugsuŋŋu pam, ka dizugu u daa yɛli, ni, u ya sɔ'ai n‑za'asi u ka niriba bu ya baŋŋi di gbinni: ni, “M bu boori ni ba ti diisi u vi.”

ref: While Joseph was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, “Joseph, the baby that Mary will have is from the Holy Spirit. Go ahead and marry her.
text: Ŋɔn daa na kur teesiri lala yɛla maa, ka u daa zaasi zaasimni, ka ti Dugma malaaka daa kyɛ u saani na n‑yɛli u, “Nyini Gyoosɛfi ŋɔn nyɛ NaaDauda ya'aŋŋa wa, di zɔri dabeem ni i kyɛ ka ba taasi Mariama i yiri na, dama ŋɔn mari puseaa maa yi la Naawunni Sii Kasi yiiko ni na.

ref: Then after her baby is born, name him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.”
text: U nin ti dɔ'ai bidibga, ka i purim u yuuri Yisa, dama u nin ti tiligi u niriba ba tumbeeri taalli zaa ni.”

ref: After Joseph woke up, he and Mary were soon married, just as the Lord's angel had told him to do.
text: Tɔ, ka sahaseaa Gyoosɛfi ŋɔn daa neaai goom ni la, u daa niŋi hoo ti Dugma malaaka maa ŋɔn sa yɛli u seem maa, ka kyɛ ka ba taasi u pɔ'a maa na n‑ti u.

ref: But they did not sleep together before her baby was born. Then Joseph named him Jesus.
text: Amaa ba daa bu la'am taaba n‑dooni halli ka Mariama ti dɔ'ai u pɔ'asaridɔ'am, bidibga; ka Gyoosɛfi daa puri u yuuri Yisa.

ref: When Jesus was born in the village of Bethlehem in Judea, Herod was king. During this time some wise men from the east came to Jerusalem
text: Ban daa ti dɔ'ai Yisa Bɛtlihɛm timpuuni n‑naai, Meegyai daa yi wuntampusiri tinwa'aligu ziiya n‑kyɛ Gyɛrusalɛm na - ban n daa nyɛ ninvugseeba ban baŋŋi saŋmariwuusi yɛla, ni bamsim pam m‑paasi. Di saha maa Bɛtlihɛm daa bɛ la Gyuudiiya naam suulinsi ni, ka NaaHɛroodi mun daa di lala tingbaŋŋu naam maa.

ref: and said, “Where is the child born to be king of the Jews? We saw his star in the east and have come to worship him.”
text: Ka Meegyai maa daa kur boosiri, ni, “Yɛni ka ba dɔ'ai Izraɛli dima naaba la? Ti daa nya u saŋmariwuu maa wuntampusiri lugri ni, dizugu ka ti kyɛ na ni ti gyeem u.”

ref: When King Herod heard about this, he was worried, and so was everyone else in Jerusalem.
text: Ka NaaHɛroodi ŋɔn daa wum ba lala boosigu maa, di daa gyɛri u pampam - ŋɔn ni u niriba ban bɛ Gyɛrusalɛm zaa - ka ba sufu daa sa'am.

ref: They told him, “He will be born in Bethlehem, just as the prophet wrote,
text: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Bɛtlihɛm tiŋŋa kan bɛ Gyuudiiya suulinsi ni la ka ba nin dɔ'ai u. Ni, dama anabidima daa sɔbi n‑zaali, ni#:-

ref: ‘Bethlehem in the land of Judea, you are very important among the towns of Judea. From your town will come a leader, who will be like a shepherd for my people Israel.’ ”
text: “Nyin Bɛtlihɛm tiŋŋa wa, Nyin bɛ Gyuudiiya suulinsi ni la, I bu poora Gyuudiiya nabiisi sunsukkuni; Nasɔ m bɛ ni ŋɔn ti ya n‑guri ya - Ya Izraɛli dima maa - Hoo ban guri bunkɔbri seem la: Ŋɔnwa ti ya n‑yi nyin Bɛtlihɛm saani na.”

ref: Herod secretly called in the wise men and asked them when they had first seen the star.
text: Ka NaaHɛroodi daa booli Meegyai maa asiiri ni, m‑maali m‑boosi ba, ni, “Bɔn saha deede ka saŋmariwuu maa daa pusi saazugu na?” Ka ba daa wulisi u di saha ŋɔn bɛ seem.

ref: He told them, “Go to Bethlehem and search carefully for the child. As soon as you find him, let me know. I also want to go and worship him.”
text: Ka u daa yɛli ba, “Ya kyɛm Bɛtlihɛm nti bɔ bii maa lahabaari. Ka ya yi ti paai n‑nya u, ya tumma yooma na n‑yɛli ma, ka man gba nin kyaŋŋi nti gyeem u.”

ref: The wise men listened to what the king said and then left. And the star they had seen in the east went on ahead of them until it stopped over the place where the child was.
text: Ka ban daa wum Naaba ŋɔn daa yɛli ba seem la, ba daa yi n‑lu Bɛtlihɛm soori yaasa, ka saŋmariwuu maa daa gaari ba tooni ka ba daa dɔla - ban daa pun nya seaa wuntampusiri zii la - halli nti paai n‑zaani saazugu n‑wulisiri bii maa ŋɔn daa bɛ seela.

ref: They were thrilled and excited to see the star.
text: Ba sufu daa maaya pam, saŋmariwuu maa zugu.

ref: Later they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, and they went back home by another road. The Escape to Egypt
text: Tɔ; ka Naawunni daa kyɛ ka ba zaasi zaasimni m‑bugsi ba, ni, ba di naa n‑labi NaaHɛroodi saani, ka dizugu ba daa lɔkki sɔseaa n‑labi n‑kuli ba tiŋŋa.

ref: After the wise men had gone, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Get up! Hurry and take the child and his mother to Egypt! Stay there until I tell you to return, because Herod is looking for the child and wants to kill him.”
text: Ban daa ti gaari n‑kuli n‑naai, Gyoosɛfi daa zaasi zaasimni n‑nya ka ti Dugma malaaka daa kyaŋŋi u saani na nti yɛli u, ni, “Doaama n‑zaŋŋi bii maa ni u ma la n‑zɔ n‑kyaŋŋi Igyipti. See ka ya kpalim m‑bɛ bɛni halli ka man ti laa n‑ti ya kukɔri; dama NaaHɛroodi ya boori la bii maa ziiya ni u gbaai u n‑ku.”

ref: That night, Joseph got up and took his wife and the child to Egypt,
text: Ka u daa doaai yuŋŋu n‑zaŋŋi u pɔ'a ni bii maa n‑yisigi n‑kyaŋŋi Igyipti.

ref: where they stayed until Herod died. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I called my son out of Egypt.” The Killing of the Children
text: Ba daa kpalim la bɛni halli ka NaaHɛroodi ti kpi. Ka dinŋɔ n daa kyɛ ka di siri niŋi hoo ti Dugma ŋɔn daa pun kyɛ ka u anabisɔ ti tɔ'asi anabitiri ni, ni#:- “N daa booli m bii maa Ka yiisi u Igyipti tingbaŋŋu la ni na.”

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Ka NaaHɛroodi ŋɔn daa ti baŋŋi, ni, Meegyai maa yɔmsi u ka sɔ'ai n‑kuli, u sufu daa yisigiya pam. Ka u daa zaali zaaligu, ni, ba kyaŋŋi Bɛtlihɛm, ni tinkpanseesi an bɛ gyilagyila la ni, nti ku biisi maa - bidibseeba ban dɔ'ai ka ba nyɛ dahinyinni halli nti paai yuuma ayi biisi la zaa. U lala zaaligu maa daa dɔlisi la sahaseaa Meegyai maa ŋɔn daa wulisi u la.

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,
text: Di saha di siri daa niŋi hoo Anabi Gyɛrimaaya ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi anabitiri yɛliseelli, ni#:-

ref: “In Ramah a voice was heard crying and weeping loudly. Rachel was mourning for her children, and she refused to be comforted, because they were dead.” The Return from Egypt
text: “Raama ka kukɔri maa yi na, Kunkuma ni fabligu pam n nyɛ di. Reekyɛl n kumni u biisi zugu Halli ka sɔ ka ni n‑nin toaai n‑goaai u; Dama u biisi maa zaa na ka ni.”

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Tɔ. Ka NaaHɛroodi kum nyaaŋŋa, Gyoosɛfi daa zaasi zaasimni, ka ti Dugma malaaka daa laa n‑kyɛ u saani na

ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: nti yɛli u, ni, “Doaama n‑zaŋŋi bii maa ni u ma la n‑labi Izraɛli tingbaŋŋu ni, dama ninvugseeba ban daa boori ba ku bii maa la kpiya.”

ref: Joseph got up and left with them for Israel.
text: Ka dizugu u daa doaai n‑zaŋŋi bii maa ni u ma la n‑labi Izraɛli tingbaŋŋu ni.

ref: But when he heard that Herod's son Archelaus was now ruler of Judea, he was afraid to go there. Then in a dream he was told to go to Galilee,
text: Amaa ŋɔn daa ti wum, ni, NaaHɛroodi bii Arikyileesi n diri u ba Hɛroodi naam n‑su Gyuudiiya maa, dabeem daa gbaai u ni u kyaŋŋi bɛni. Ka u daa zaasi zaasimni ka di wulisi u, ni, u kyɛm Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni nti zinni bɛni.

ref: and they went to live there in the town of Nazareth. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “He will be called a Nazarene.”
text: Ka u daa doaai n‑kyaŋŋi n‑ti zinni Nazarɛti tiŋŋa ni. Ka dinŋɔ daa kyɛ ka di siri daa niŋi hoo anabidima ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi seem n‑zaali la, ni#:- “Ba ti ya m‑boonni u la Nazarɛti nira.”

ref: He said, “Turn back to God! The kingdom of heaven will soon be here.”
text: n‑yɛtti, ni, “Ya kyɛli ya teesibeeri ni ya tumbeeri zaa, dama Arizana naam bɛ la ya saani.”

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Ŋɔn Gyɔni daa yeeri la arakoomi kɔbri bunyeerigu, ka daa mari gbaŋŋu n‑loori u seaa, ka u bundirigu daa nyɛ toori, ni siiri.

ref: From Jerusalem and all Judea and from the Jordan River Valley crowds of people went to John.
text: Ka daadam pam daa la'am n‑yi n‑kyɛ u saani na; ba daa yi la Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni na, ni Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa ni, halli ni tinseesi zaa an bɛ Gyɔdin mɔ'ari duuri m‑paasi.

ref: They told how sorry they were for their sins, and he baptized them in the river.
text: Ka ba daa paai u na ka wulisiri ban tum tumbɛseesi maa, ka u daa su ba tumbeeri kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi kɔsuubu, Gyɔdin mɔ'ari la ni.

ref: An ax is ready to cut the trees down at their roots. Any tree that doesn't produce good fruit will be chopped down and thrown into a fire.
text: Pampaŋŋɔ ka Naawunni nin namsi tumbeeri dima hoo nira ŋɔn maali u leefu siri ni u ŋma tiseaa kan bu wanni suŋŋasuŋŋa seem. U nin ŋma ka n‑lusi n‑zaŋŋi ka n‑lɔsi bugum ni.”

ref: His threshing fork is in his hand, and he is ready to separate the wheat from the husks. He will store the wheat in a barn and burn the husks in a fire that never goes out. The Baptism of Jesus (Mark
text: U kyɛnni na; ka pun maali u neema siri ni u pii u kabuubu tampii maa ka ka niŋi kasikasi. Ka u nin yeeli u kyi maa n‑yiisi wuurigu la ka kyɛ kabeelim. Kasuma maa ka u nin la'asi n‑su kusɔkku maa ni, ka naayi zaŋŋi wuurigu maa zaa n‑vaai m‑basi bugunseebu bun ka kpiisigu puuni.”

ref: But John kept objecting and said, “I ought to be baptized by you. Why have you come to me?”
text: Amaa Gyɔni daa bu sakki, ka yɛli u, “Mani n da naa n‑kyɛ ka nyini su ma koom, ka nyin laa n‑kyɛnni n saani na, bee?”

ref: Jesus answered, “For now this is how it should be, because we must do all God wants us to do.” Then John agreed.
text: Ka Yisa daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli u, ni, “Kur kyɛ ka di niŋi lala pampaŋŋɔa. Dama di sumsi ni ti tumni bunseelli kam zaa la Naawunni ŋɔn zaali n‑ti ti seem maa.” Ka di saha maa ka Gyɔni daa sakki ka daa su u koom maa.

ref: So Jesus was baptized. And as soon as he came out of the water, the sky opened, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down on him like a dove.
text: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti deaai Naawunni kɔsuubu n‑naai ka daa ti yi koom maa ni na, di saha maa ka sangbana daa yoaai ka u daa nya ka Naawunni Sii daa sigri u zugu na; Sii maa mun daa ŋmani la hoo naŋmanni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

ref: Then a voice from heaven said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”
text: Ka ba daa wum Naawunni kukɔri saazugu na, n‑yɛtti, ni, “M bii nŋɔa, ka n wuri u ni pam. N nini n tigi u.”

ref: When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, he went to Galilee.
text: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti wum, ni, ba gbaai Gyɔni n‑kpari, u daa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi m‑paai Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni.

ref: But instead of staying in Nazareth, Jesus moved to Capernaum. This town was beside Lake Galilee in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali.
text: U daa bu kpalim Nazarɛti tiŋŋa, amaa u daa kyaŋŋi nti bɛ Kapirinyum tiŋŋa ni, tinseaa kan zɛ mɔ'ari gbiriŋŋu ni, Zɛbulɔn ni Naftaali tingbana ziiya.

ref: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
text: Ka Sintaani, yɔmsigu daana, daa kyɛ u saani na n‑yɛli u, ni, “I yi siri nyɛ la Naawunni biiya, i ku kyɛ ka kuga wa leebi bundira?”

ref: Jesus answered, “The Scriptures also say, ‘Don't try to test the Lord your God!’ ”
text: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, “Ka di laa n‑sɔbi m‑paasi, ni, nyin di zamsi i Dugma Naawunni!”

ref: Finally, the devil took Jesus up on a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms on earth and their power.
text: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Sintaani daa zaŋŋi u n‑kyaŋŋi n‑duusi n‑tɔm zɔwa'alli saazugu m‑bulisi u duniya puuni tinsi zaasa, ni a gyirima maa.

ref: The devil said to him, “I will give all this to you, if you will bow down and worship me.”
text: Ka yɛli u, ni, “I yi ti gbaani tiŋŋa n‑gyeem ma Naawunni gyeeŋŋu, n nin zaŋŋi anŋɔ zaa n‑ti i.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Go away Satan! The Scriptures say: ‘Worship the Lord your God and serve only him.’ ”
text: Ka Yisa yɛli u, ni, “Kyɛma sa, Sintaani! Di pun sɔbi n‑dɔya, ni#:- ‘Naawunni yinni kɔkɔa, Ŋɔn nyɛ ya Dugma la, Ŋɔn sumsi gbaanigu ni gyeeŋŋu.’ ”

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said,
text: Dinŋɔ daa kyɛ ka anabi Aizaaya ŋɔn daa yɛli seem la niŋi yɛlimaŋni, ni#:-

ref: “Listen, lands of Zebulun and Naphtali, lands along the road to the sea and across the Jordan. Listen Galilee, land of the Gentiles!
text: “Zɛbulɔn tingbaŋŋu, wumma! Naftaali tingbaŋŋu, wumma! Yan bɛ teeku la soori maa, Yan bɛ Gyɔdin mɔ'ari ya'ari sa: Yan bɛ Galilii, saamma tiŋŋa la ni, gba, wumma!#:-

ref: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
text: Ka ba daa basi ba laama maa yoomyooma ka dɔli u.

ref: While Jesus was walking along the shore of Lake Galilee, he saw two brothers. One was Simon, also known as Peter, and the other was Andrew. They were fishermen, and they were casting their net into the lake.
text: Ka Yisa daa kyɛnni n‑dɔli la Galilii mɔ'ari duuri la nti nya niriba ayi, Saimoon ni u tunzɔ Anduru, ka ba daa lɔbri ba laama mɔ'ari maa ni, dama ba daa tumni la zingbaariba tuma.

ref: Jesus walked on until he saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat with their father, mending their nets. Jesus asked them to come with him.
text: Ŋɔn daa yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi tooni, u daa nya niriba ayi yaasa m‑paasi, Gyeemsi, Zɛbidii biiya, ni u tunzɔ Gyɔni; ka ban ni ba ba Zɛbidii daa bɛ nyariŋŋu ni m‑maanni ba laama. Ka Yisa daa booli ba ni ba dɔli u na.

ref: At once they left the boat and their father and went with Jesus. Jesus Teaches, Preaches, and Heals (Luke
text: Di saha ka ba daa bakki ka kyɛ nyariŋŋu maa, halli n‑kyɛ ba ba Zɛbidii gba, ka kyaŋŋi n‑dɔli Yisa nyaaŋŋa.

ref: Jesus went all over Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and preaching the good news about God's kingdom. He also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
text: Ka u daa gyiligi Galilii tinsi zaa n‑kpeeri ba la'aŋŋu duri ni n‑wulisiri ka moonni lahabasuŋŋu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Arizana naam yɛla, ka tibri niriba ban daa beeri zaa bɛni ka dooru balimbalim m mari ba, bee, ban kɔŋŋni yaa ni alaafɛa.

ref: News about him spread all over Syria, and people with every kind of sickness or disease were brought to him. Some of them had a lot of demons in them, others were thought to be crazy, and still others could not walk. But Jesus healed them all.
text: Tɔ; ka lahabaari zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi u yɛla daa paai halli Siriiya tingbaŋŋu ni sa, ka niriba daa mari ba baaridima zaa n‑kyɛ u saani na, dooru ni beerim kam zaa ŋɔn mari seeba la, ni arizimbeeri ŋɔn daa kpɛ seeba, ni kpisinkpiiri ŋɔn daa gbaai seeba, ni ban nuusi bee ban gbaya n daa kpi la. Ka Yisa daa tibi ba zaa.

ref: Large crowds followed Jesus from Galilee and the region around the ten cities known as Decapolis. They also came from Jerusalem, Judea, and from across the Jordan River.
text: Ka Galilii dima daadam pam daa yi na n‑dɔli u, ni Tinsi Piiya tingbaŋŋu dima, ni Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa, ni Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu dima, ni tingbanseaa zaa an bɛ Gyɔdin mɔ'ari ya'ari wa sa.

ref: When Jesus saw the crowds, he went up on the side of a mountain and sat down. Blessings (Luke
text: Tɔ. Yisa ŋɔn daa nya niriba maa, u daa kyaŋŋi nti du zoori la ni. U daa zinni bɛni ka u nyaandɔliba daa kyɛ u saani na.

ref: You must obey God's commands better than the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law obey them. If you don't, I promise you will never get into the kingdom of heaven. Anger
text: Dama man ya n‑yɛli ya wa, ni, di yi ti pa la ya dɔli Naawunni soori suŋŋa n‑gaari zaaligu karimbadima ban dɔli seem maa, ni Farisidima ŋɔn dɔli seem maa, ya ku nya sɔseaa yan nin zaŋŋi n‑kpɛ Arizana naam ziiya.”

ref: So if you are about to place your gift on the altar and remember that someone is angry with you,
text: Dizugu i yi ti mari i piini n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai maaligu ziiya, di yi pa seem ka bɛni ka i ti teaai, ni, i niŋi i mabisɔ taalli.

ref: and he taught them:
text: Ka u daa gbaai ba wulisigu n‑yɛli, ni#:-

ref: God blesses those people who depend only on him. They belong to the kingdom of heaven!
text: “Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ninvugseeba ban nyɛ faradima ba sufu ni, dama Arizana naam nyɛ la ba dinni.

ref: God blesses those people who grieve. They will find comfort!
text: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ninvugseeba ban sufu n sa'amna, dama Naawunni nin kyɛ ka ba sufu maai.

ref: God blesses those people who are humble. The earth will belong to them!
text: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ninvugseeba ban sigsi ba mansi, dama ba nin di faari n‑su duniya wa.

ref: God blesses those people who are merciful. They will be treated with mercy!
text: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ninvugseeba ban zɔri niriba namboo dama Naawunni nin zɔ ba gba namboo.

ref: God blesses those people whose hearts are pure. They will see him!
text: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ninvugseeba ban mari sufu yinni, dama ba nin ti nya Naawunni.

ref: You are the light for the whole world. A city built on top of a hill cannot be hidden,
text: Yaasa, ya ŋmanni la neesim, ni duniya zaa ti neaai suŋŋa. Tinseaa yi mɛ n‑zɛ zoori zugu, ka ku toaai n‑laa n‑sɔ'ai.

ref: But I tell you not to swear by anything when you make a promise! Heaven is God's throne, so don't swear by heaven.
text: Amaa man yɛtti ya wa, ni, yan di pɔ noori halli beela. Di pɔ arizana, dama Naawunni naam zizii nla.

ref: The earth is God's footstool, so don't swear by the earth. Jerusalem is the city of the great king, so don't swear by it.
text: Mun di pɔ tingbaŋŋu, dama Naawunni napɔntɔmnigu nla. Di laa m‑pɔ Gyɛrusalɛm, dama Naaba Yiikodaana maa tiŋŋa nla.

ref: You are the salt for everyone on earth. But if salt no longer tastes like salt, how can it make food salty? All it is good for is to be thrown out and walked on.
text: Ni, “Ya ŋmanni la yaarim, ni duniya zaa ti nya'asa. Amaa yaarim nya'asim yi ti yi ka kyɛ di, di laa n‑ya n‑niŋi la wula ka di nya'asim maa labi na yaasa? Di saha maa di laa n‑ka anfaani, ka ba yi zaŋŋi di ni m‑basi samanni ni, ka niriba yi kyɛnni n‑noori di.

ref: and no one lights a lamp and puts it under a clay pot. Instead, it is placed on a lampstand, where it can give light to everyone in the house.
text: Sɔ na zi n‑nyoaai firila n‑zaŋŋi ka n‑sɔ'ai luginseaa, bee n‑zaŋŋi kuriba m‑pili ka; see ka u na'a yi zaŋŋi ka ni, n‑tɔm bunseelli zugu ni ban kam zaa bɛ duu maa ni nin nya kan neaai seem maa.

ref: Don't suppose I came to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I did not come to do away with them, but to give them their full meaning.
text: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, “Ya di teesi, ni, n kyɛnni na ni n ti sa'am Musa zaaligu ni anabidima la bulisigu yɛla. Ay! N bu kyɛ na ni n ti sa'am a ni, amaa ni n ti kyɛ ka a niŋi yɛlimaŋni.

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Do not murder” and “A murderer must be brought to trial.”
text: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Tɔ; ya pun wum, ni, ba daa yɛli ti yaadima, ni, ‘Di ku ninsaala.’, ka sɔ ŋɔn ku nin ti bɛ sariya karibu yɛttɔ'a ni.

ref: But I promise you if you are angry with someone, you will have to stand trial. If you call someone a fool, you will be taken to court. And if you say that someone is worthless, you will be in danger of the fires of hell.
text: Amaa man yɛtti ya wa, ni, ŋɔn suuri yisigi n‑niŋi u mabiiya, u bɛ la sariya karibu ni. Ka ŋɔn mun ti laa n‑yɛli u mabiiya, ‘Nyin bunkyɔ'aŋŋu wa!’, ba nin boosi u boosa gyintooriba duu maa ni. Ka ŋɔn mun ti laa n‑yɛli u mabiiya, ‘Nyin gyɛrigu wa!’, u nin di wahala bugum ni.

ref: leave your gift there in front of the altar. Make peace with that person, then come back and offer your gift to God.
text: Di yi bɛ lala, see ka i kyɛ i piini maa n‑dooli maaligu zii la ni ka kyaŋŋi m‑maali yɛttɔ'a maa ka nyin ni i mabii maa nya nangbanyinni pɔi, ka naayi labi na n‑tisi.

ref: Before you are dragged into court, make friends with the person who has accused you of doing wrong. If you don't, you will be handed over to the judge and then to the officer who will put you in jail.
text: M‑paasi, nyin ni sɔ yi mari sariya karibu yɛttɔ'a ka nyin ni ŋɔn ti bɛ soori ni n‑kyɛnni sariya karibu ziiya, kur kpaŋŋma i maŋŋa n‑kyɛ ka ya maali yɛttɔ'a maa yooma, lala saha maa; di yi pa lala u nin zaŋŋi i n‑niŋi sarikatta nuu ni, ka ŋɔn mun nin zaŋŋi i n‑niŋi pirinsi nuu ni, ka ba nin kpari i dansarika duu ni.

ref: I promise you will not get out until you have paid the last cent you owe. Marriage
text: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti i wa, i ku laa n‑yi bɛni na see ka i ti yɔ i samni maa zaa n‑naai, halli ka kɔbɔ yinni bu kpalim gba.”

ref: You have been taught that a man who divorces his wife must write out divorce papers for her.
text: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, “Ba daa yɛli, ni, ‘Dɔsɔ yi ya n‑kari u pɔ'a, see ka u sɔbi pɔ'akaribu gbaŋŋu n‑ti u.’

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “M‑paasi yaasa, ya pun wum, ni, ba daa yɛli ti yaadima, ni, ‘Di pɔ noori ka yi di nyaaŋŋa; i yi ti pɔ, ni, i zaŋŋi bunseelli n‑ti Naawunni, see ka i siri zaŋŋi n‑tisi.’

ref: When you make a promise, say only “Yes” or “No.” Anything else comes from the devil. Revenge (Luke
text: Kur tɔ'asiri n‑yɛtti ‘Ii, ii!’, bee, ‘Ay, ay!’, dama din paasi lala zugu yi la Yɛlibeeri Daana la saani na.”

ref: But I tell you not to try to get even with a person who has done something to you. When someone slaps your right cheek, turn and let that person slap your other cheek.
text: Amaa man yɛtti ya wa, ni, yan di zabi ni ya ta'ai ya taalli daana tuma; faasee ka sɔ yi lɔbi i tampɛkka, nyin ŋmaligma kanla gba m‑paasi u.

ref: If someone sues you for your shirt, give up your coat as well.
text: Sɔ yi ti ya taasi i kootu ni n‑deaai i bunyeerigu, ka laa m‑boori i kyinkyinni m‑paasi, nyin di mɔŋŋi u.

ref: If a soldier forces you to carry his pack one kilometer, carry it two kilometers.
text: Ka sɔ yi ya mugsi i, ni, i zi u neema n‑kyaŋŋi maalli yinni, nyin ziima a n‑kyaŋŋi abayi n‑ti u.

ref: When people ask you for something, give it to them. When they want to borrow money, lend it to them. Love (Luke
text: Ninvugsɔ yi ti susi bunni i saani, zaŋŋma n‑ti u. Sɔ mun boori ni i pɛŋŋi u bunseelli, nyin di mɔŋŋi u.”

ref: You have heard people say, “Love your neighbors and hate your enemies.”
text: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Tɔ; ya pun wum, ni, ba daa yɛli, ni, i wuri i zɔ ka yaai i dindaana.

ref: But I tell you to love your enemies and pray for anyone who mistreats you.
text: Amaa man yɛtti ya, ni, ya wura ya dindaandima, ka susiri Naawunni n‑tiri ban namsiri ya.

ref: Then you will be acting like your Father in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both good and bad people. And he sends rain for the ones who do right and for the ones who do wrong.
text: Lala ka ya nin ti leebi bisuma n‑ti ya Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni. Ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn kyɛ ka wuntaŋŋa pusiri n‑neaari ninvugbeeri m‑paasi ninvugsuma zugu; ka laa n‑kyɛ ka saa miiri n‑niŋni ban deaari u yɛla ni ban za'asiri u yɛla m‑paasi.

ref: If you love only those people who love you, will God reward you for this? Even tax collectors love their friends.
text: Ya yi wuri la ban wuri ya, ya nin nya laara di zugu? Ay! Halli lampodeaariba gba niŋni la lala - bee, m ma'ari ni?

ref: If you greet only your friends, what's so great about this? Don't even unbelievers do that?
text: Ka ya yi puusiri la ya mabiisi kɔkɔa, tunsuŋŋu bɔ ka ya tumna? Halli ban pa Izraɛli dima gba niŋni lala, bee, m ma'ari ni?

ref: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
text: I yi ya puusi Naawunni, di niŋi hoo birikɔnsi dima: ba boori ba zɛ saazugu m‑puusiri gyeeŋŋu duri ni, ni sɔkyira ni, ni niriba ti nya ba. Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ba pun deaai ba laara zaa n‑naai.

ref: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
text: Nyin muna, i yi ya puusi Naawunni, nyin kpeem i duu puuni n‑yɔ kyɛburu la ka sɔ'ai m‑puusi n‑ti i Ba. Ka i Ba ŋɔn nyari yɛlisɔ'ara zaa nin yɔ i.

ref: When you pray, don't talk on and on as people do who don't know God. They think God likes to hear long prayers.
text: Tɔ: ya yi ya m‑puusiri Naawunni, di mari yɛttɔ'ayɔna pam hoo ban pa Izraɛli dima ŋɔn niŋni seem. Dama ba teesi, ni, ba yi ti tɔ'asi n‑yuui, ni, Naawunni nin wum n‑ti ba dizugu.

ref: Don't be like them. Your Father knows what you need even before you ask.
text: Yarima muna, yan di ŋmanni bana. Dama ya Ba pun mi yan boori seelli pɔi, ka ya naayi susi.

ref: You should pray like this: Our Father in heaven, help us to honor your name.
text: Dinŋɔ ka ya nin puusi, ni#:- ‘Ti Ba, nyin bɛ la arizana ni, Kyɛ ka i yuuri du.

ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
text: Kyɛ ka i naam kyɛ na; Kyɛ ka ba tumni i yuubu yɛla N‑niŋi duniya wa ni, Hoo ban niŋni arizana ni seem.

ref: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
text: Dama ya yi kyɛ ya taaba tumbeeri taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ba, ya Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana la gba nin kyɛ ya mammansi taalli m‑pɛŋŋi.

ref: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating
text: Amaa ya yi bu kyɛ ya taaba taalli m‑pɛŋŋi, dinŋɔna ya Ba maa gba ku kyɛ ya mammansi taalli m‑pɛŋŋi.”

ref: Then others won't know you are going without eating. But your Father sees what is done in private, and he will reward you. Treasures in Heaven (Luke
text: ka lala ka ninsaalidima ku baŋŋi i noori loobu yɛla, see ka i Ba ŋɔn bɛ la zisɔ'arli maa ni nin baŋŋi. Di saha ka i Ba ŋɔn nyari yɛlisɔ'ara nin yɔ i.”

ref: But more than anything else, put God's work first and do what he wants. Then the other things will be yours as well.
text: Ay, see ka ya daŋŋi n‑zaŋŋi ya Ba naam yɛttɔ'a, ni u yɛla zaa, m‑mugsi ya mansi pɔi, ka ŋɔn nin naayi zaŋŋi bunseelli din kpalim zaasa m‑paasi ya.

ref: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.
text: Di fabinni beoo yɛla zuna, see ka i kyɛ ka beoo maa sa ya fabli ka maŋtoori yɛla. Zuna mun mari la ka toori yɛlimugsa ka a sakki.”

ref: Instead, store up your treasures in heaven, where moths and rust cannot destroy them, and thieves cannot break in and steal them.
text: Amaa ya la'asim neenseesi an nin zaali arizana ni n‑niŋi ya arizikki maa; bɛni ka leelisi ku ŋɔbi a, bee, a ku nyaamna, ka na'ayigsi mun gba ku toaai n‑kabri n‑zuuri a.

ref: Your heart will always be where your treasure is. Light (Luke
text: Dama i sufu kur ya bɛ la i arizikki ŋɔn bɛ seela.”

ref: But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark. If the light inside you is dark, you surely are in the dark. Money (Luke
text: Amaa i nini yi sa'amna, i ningbina zaa bɛ la zibsim ni. Tɔ - ka neesim din bɛ i ni maa yi nyɛ la zibsim, di nin zibgi halli nti laa n‑ya'ai.”

ref: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
text: Ni, “Ninvugyinni ku toaai n‑leebi daabri n‑ti dugmadima ayi: see ka u za'asi yinni yɛla ka wuri yinni la, bee u tibgi yinni la, ka laa n‑zeem ŋɔnla. Ya ku toaai n‑kyɛ ka Naawunni su ya yɛla ka kyɛ ka ligri gba su ya.”

ref: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
text: Dinzugu yan di fabinna, bee n‑teesiri, ni, ‘Bɔ ka ti ya di?’, bee, ‘Bɔ ka ti ya nyu?’, bee, ‘Bɔ ka ti ya yɛa?’

ref: I tell you not to worry about your life. Don't worry about having something to eat, drink, or wear. Isn't life more than food or clothing?
text: Ni, “Dizugu ka n ya yɛli ya, ni, yan di zaŋŋi yɛla m‑mugsi ya mansi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ya nin niŋi seem n‑nya bundirigu n‑di, bee n‑nya situra n‑yɛa - yan di fabli anŋɔ zaa yɛla; dama nyɔvuri gaari bundirigu, ka ningbina gaari bunyeerigu, bee m ma'ari ni?

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Ya gɔsim niinsi ban bɛ saazugu la; ba bu butta, ba mun bu la'asiri bundirigu n‑niŋni napaari ni, amaa ya Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni la diisiri ba ni: ka yan mun mari bukaata n‑gaari ba, bee m ma'ari ni?

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: Ka wula zugu ka ya zaŋŋi bunyeera yɛla m‑mugsiri ya mansi? Ya baŋŋma pɔ'a-veelli-ku-siisi-koom la yɛla: gɔsim n‑nya kan zoaari seem moo ni, ka bu tumni n‑waanni gumni.

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: Amaa n yɛtti ya wa, ni, halli NaaSulɛmaana ni u naam gyirima zaasa gba daa na zi m‑bɔbi suturiveela kaman mɔpuuma wa yinni veelim ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
text: Ka Naawunni yi zaŋŋi veelim wa tataabu n‑niŋi mɔseesi an bɛ ni zuna ka beoo ka a sa nin nyɔ bugum m‑basi maa, wula zugu ka u ku yeeli yarima sutura? - Ya yɛdda kɔŋŋniba wa!

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
text: Ninvugseeba ban bu mi Naawunni la bɛ la anŋɔ zaa nɔba karibu ni. Amaa yarima mari ya Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni maa, ka u mi halli, ni, ya boori la anŋɔ zaa.

ref: God will be as hard on you as you are on others! He will treat you exactly as you treat them.
text: Ka yan ma'asiri n‑tiri ya taaba seem, lala ka Naawunni nin ti ma'asi n‑ti ya gba.”

ref: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
text: U daa laa m‑boosi ba, ni, “Ni wula ka i nya muŋŋa i mabii nimbiri ni ka zaantɔliga dee n‑sa i maŋtoori dinni ni ka i bu nya ka?

ref: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye?
text: Bee wula ka i nin toaai n‑yɛli i mabiiya, ni, u kyɛ ka i yiisi munseaa kan bɛ u nimbiri ni, ka zaantɔliga maa kpalim m‑bɛ i maŋtoori dinni ni?

ref: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
text: Nyin birikɔnsi daana wa! Nyin daŋŋma n‑voaai i nimbiri ni zaantɔliga maa m‑basi ka nyari suŋŋa pɔi, ka naayi yiisi munseaa kan bɛ i mabii nimbiri ni!”

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
text: Tɔ! ya nyɛ la ninvugseeba bam mari teesibeeri ya beebu ni, ka halli yarima gba yi mi yan zaŋŋi piinisuma n‑tiri ya biisi; ka dinŋɔna ya Ba ŋɔn bɛ saazugu ku zaŋŋi u piinisuma n‑ti ban susiri u?”

ref: Would any of you give your hungry child a stone, if the child asked for some bread?
text: Sɔ m bɛ ya puuni ka u biiya nin susi paanu ka u mɔŋŋi, ka zaŋŋi kugri n‑ti u? - Ay!

ref: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened for you.
text: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, “Ya susima ka nin deaai yan susi seelli maa; ya booma ka nin nya yan boori seelli maa; ya kpaasima ka ba nin yoaai kyɛburu maa n‑ti ya.

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Dama ŋɔnkam susira deaari ni; ŋɔnkam boora nyari ni; ka ŋɔnkam kpaasira, ba nin yoaai n‑ti u.

ref: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
text: Bee, u yi ti susi ziŋŋa ka u mɔŋŋi ka zaŋŋi waafu n‑ti u? - Ay!

ref: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
text: Ni, “Dinŋɔna yan boori ni niriba niŋi seem n‑ti ya zaa, see ka ya niŋi lala n‑ti ba gba. Zaaligu yɛla maa zaa nla, ni anabidima maa bulisigu m‑paasi.”

ref: Anyone who hears and obeys these teachings of mine is like a wise person who built a house on solid rock.
text: Ni, “Dizugu ŋɔnkam zaa wum n yɛligu ka dɔli ka, u ŋmanni la yam daansɔ ŋɔn daa mɛ u yiri n‑tɔm tampii zugu.

ref: But the gate to life is very narrow. The road that leads there is so hard to follow that only a few people find it. A Tree and Its Fruit (Luke
text: Sɔseaa n bɛ ni, ka ka za'anɔri poora, ka soori maa bu yɛlima; ka mun wa la nyɔvuri ziiya, amaa niriba ban nya ka n‑dɔla bu zoaai.”

ref: Watch out for false prophets! They dress up like sheep, but inside they are wolves who have come to attack you.
text: Ni, “Ya baŋŋma, ka ziri anabidima m bɛ ni. Ba ŋmani la kunduna ban yeeri peesi gbana, ni ba ti la'am ni peesi maa n‑gbaai ba.

ref: You can tell what they are by what they do. No one picks grapes or figs from thornbushes.
text: Ba wala nin kyɛ ka ya baŋŋi ba. Goaa bu wanni sisibi ka kpikpaliga mun ku toaai n‑wali kinkama.

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit.
text: Tiseaa kan mari alaafɛ wanni la walisuma. Tiseaa kan ka alaafɛ muna - walibeeri ka ka nin wali.

ref: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
text: Tiseaa kan mari alaafɛ ku toaai n‑wali walibeeri, halli ka kan ka alaafɛ ku toaai n‑wali bunsuma.

ref: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
text: Tiseelli kam kan bu wanni walisuma, ba nin ŋmaai ka m‑basi, ka zaŋŋi ka n‑niŋi bugum ni.

ref: You can tell who the false prophets are by their deeds. A Warning (Luke
text: Lala zugu ka n yɛliya, ni, ba wala - di nyɛ la ba tuma maa - nin kyɛ ka ya baŋŋi ba.”

ref: Anyone who hears my teachings and doesn't obey them is like a foolish person who built a house on sand.
text: Ŋɔnkam zaa mun wum n yɛligu ka bu dɔli ka, u ŋmanni la gyɛrigu sɔ ŋɔn daa mɛ u yiri n‑tɔm tambisigu ni.

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and the winds blew and beat against that house. Finally, it fell with a crash.
text: Saa daa mi pam, ka mɔ'a daa paali n‑yakki m‑pɔbri na, ka pɛbsigu daa dam pam. Ka yiri maa daa lu zaa, ka ka luuwa daa bu veela gba.”

ref: Not everyone who calls me their Lord will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only the ones who obey my Father in heaven will get in.
text: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Pa seeba ban boonni ma, ni, ‘N Dugma, n Dugma’ zaa n nin kpɛ arizana naam ziiya, amaa seeba ban tumni m Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni la yuubu maa nin ti kpɛa.

ref: On the day of judgment many will call me their Lord. They will say, “We preached in your name, and in your name we forced out demons and worked many miracles.”
text: Dabsiri maa yi ti paai na, niriba pam nin ti yɛli ma, ni, ‘Ti Dugma, ti Dugma! I yuuri ni ka ti daa yɛli anabitiri yɛla; i yuuri ni ka ti daa kari arizimbeeri m‑basi; i yuuri ni ka ti daa tum alahazibu tuma pam! Bee, ti ma'ari ni?’

ref: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
text: Di saha maa ka n nin mooli n‑yɛli ba, ni, ‘Man zi ya kɔtaa! Ya zɔma ka kyɛ ma, yan tumbeeri dima wa!’ ”

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and winds beat against that house. But it was built on solid rock, and so it did not fall.
text: Saa daa mi pam, ka mɔ'a daa paali n‑yakki m‑pɔbri na, ka pɛbsigu daa dam pam. Ka di zaa yɔri, yiri maa daa bu lu, ka nyeeba ŋɔn tɔm tampii maa ni zugu.

ref: When Jesus finished speaking, the crowds were surprised at his teaching.
text: Tɔ: ka Yisa ŋɔn daa yɛli anŋɔ zaa n‑naai maa, di daa niŋi daadam maa alahazibu pam u yɛttɔ'a maa zugu.

ref: He taught them like someone with authority, and not like their teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: Dama u daa tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'amaŋni, ka u bu birisira halli beela; ni, di pa la kaman ba zaaligu baŋŋniba la ŋɔn yi wulisiri seem la.

ref: But the ones who should have been in the kingdom will be thrown out into the dark. They will cry and grit their teeth in pain.”
text: Halli ka seeba ban nyɛ naam zuliya dima maa, ba nin yiisi ba m‑basi, ni ba ti paai wa'aligu ya'aseelli ban ŋɔn bɛ zibsim ni n‑kumna ka ŋɔbri ba nyina.”

ref: That evening many people with demons in them were brought to Jesus. And with only a word he forced out the evil spirits and healed everyone who was sick.
text: Di daari zaanɔyuŋŋu, ba daa mari niriba pam ka arizimbeeri bɛ ba zugri ni, n‑kyɛ u saani na; ka u daa yɛli u yɛligu kɔkɔa, ka di katti arizimbeeri maa m‑basira, ka tibri baaridima zaasa.

ref: Suddenly a man with leprosy came and knelt in front of Jesus. He said, “Lord, you have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
text: Di saha ka kɔnsɔ daa bɛ ni, ka u kyɛ Yisa saani na n‑sɔmni u tooni n‑yɛli u, “N daana, i yi da sakki, i naa kyɛ ka n kɔmni wa naai ka n kpɛ n‑taabi ni.”

ref: Jesus put his hand on the man and said, “I want to! Now you are well.” At once the man's leprosy disappeared.
text: Ka Yisa daa teaai u nuu n‑siisi u, ka yɛli, “N sakkiya, di naama!” ka di saha maa ka u kɔmni maa daa basi u ka u daa niŋi kasi.

ref: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.
text: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, “Tɔ! N nin kyɛ i yiri maa ni na n‑tibi u.”

ref: When Jesus heard this, he was so surprised that he turned and said to the crowd following him, “I tell you in all of Israel I've never found anyone with this much faith!
text: Tɔ; ka Yisa ŋɔn daa wum toondaana maa ŋɔn daa yɛli seem maa ka di daa gyɛri u pam, ka u yɛli ban daa dɔli u maa, ni, “Yɛlimaŋni ka n ya yɛli ya, halli Izraɛli dima ni gba n na zi n‑nya sɔsɔ ŋɔn niŋi yɛdda hoo doo wa ŋɔn niŋi seem!”

ref: “Lord, my servant is at home in such terrible pain that he can't even move.”
